understanding.function: Nuclear fuel reprocessing system for extracting fissile material (uranium and plutonium) from spent nuclear fuel waste, capable of processing multiple fuel types (UOX, MOX, ERU/REU) with 1800 tHM/year capacity
understanding.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel reprocessing and fissile material recovery
constraints: Product purity standards for downstream fuel fabrication
uncertainties: Specific technical processes and equipment details not defined
Exact safety and regulatory requirements beyond ALARA principle unclear
Interface specifications with upstream waste reception and downstream fuel fabrication systems undefined
Detailed operational parameters and control systems not specified
Emergency response and accident scenarios not described
Waste management for non-recoverable materials not addressed

candidates.name: PUREX Process with Complexant-Based Stripping
candidates.description: Advanced PUREX process using complexant-based stripping for uranium/plutonium separation in nuclear fuel reprocessing
candidates.function_alignment: Direct alignment - provides uranium and plutonium extraction from spent nuclear fuel using proven industrial process
candidates.notes: Recent developments in 2005, addresses core separation requirements for fissile material recovery

ranked.name: PUREX Process with Complexant-Based Stripping
ranked.description: Advanced PUREX process using complexant-based stripping for uranium/plutonium separation in nuclear fuel reprocessing
ranked.notes: PUREX is proven industrial technology at required scale. Complexant-based improvements enhance separation efficiency for multiple fuel types.

ranked.name: Spent Fuel Dissolution and Reprocessing Processes
ranked.description: Comprehensive approach to spent fuel dissolution and reprocessing including multiple process steps
ranked.notes: Complete reprocessing workflow addresses all core functions. Established technology capable of 1800 tHM/year capacity.

ranked.name: Solvent Extraction Systems for Nuclear Power
ranked.description: Solvent extraction technologies specifically designed for nuclear power applications
ranked.notes: Core separation technology for fissile material recovery. Industrial-scale systems meet capacity and multi-fuel requirements.

ranked.name: Hydro-Process Chemical Separation
ranked.description: Chemical basis for separating recycling materials using hydro-processes in nuclear applications
ranked.notes: Provides chemical foundation for aqueous reprocessing. Recent development may require integration with proven industrial systems.

ranked.name: Process Intensification with Small Channel Technology
ranked.description: Alternative flowsheet using small channels for process intensification in spent nuclear fuel reprocessing
ranked.notes: Promising for efficiency but unproven at 1800 tHM/year scale. Requires significant development for industrial implementation.

ranked.name: Organic Reductant Systems
ranked.description: Organic reductants for Pu and Np ions in wet technology for spent nuclear fuel reprocessing
ranked.notes: Supporting technology essential for plutonium purification. Must be integrated with primary separation systems.

ranked.name: Radioactive Iodine and Krypton Control Systems
ranked.description: Control systems for radioactive iodine and krypton in nuclear fuel reprocessing facilities
ranked.notes: Critical for ALARA compliance but does not achieve primary function of fissile material extraction. Supporting technology only.

ranked.name: Treatment of Spent Radioactive Organic Solvents
ranked.description: Advanced treatment systems for spent radioactive organic solvents from nuclear fuel reprocessing plants
ranked.notes: Addresses waste management but not core fissile material recovery function. Supporting technology for secondary waste streams.
